_: Notable planetary & solar-system events 2026–2030, oriented to an observer at Larkmont (lat 34.0°N, lon -40.0°E, ~UTC-3). Each: {date(ISO, local calendar date), type, title_he, body, visible_from_larkmont(bool|'partial'), basis, confidence, note_he}. type ∈ {opposition, conjunction, greatest_elongation, eclipse_solar, eclipse_lunar, transit, occultation, meteor_storm_candidate}.
source: Derived from standard ephemeris predictions: planetary oppositions/elongations from JPL/HORIZONS-class almanac data; eclipses from NASA Five Millennium Canon of Solar/Lunar Eclipses (Espenak/Meeus). Dates given in local calendar terms.
license: Eclipse circumstances are public-domain NASA/Espenak data; planetary event dates are standard almanac values.
honesty: Eclipse dates/paths are MEASURED-grade predictions (high confidence, computed celestial mechanics). Planetary opposition/elongation dates are PREDICTED from ephemerides (high confidence on date, ±1 day). 'visible_from_larkmont' is assessed from path/altitude geometry; partial = only part of the event or a low-altitude view. This list is a curated subset of headline events, NOT exhaustive; do not treat absence as 'nothing happens'.

events.note_he: Saturn at its closest and brightest for the year, visible all night. The rings are at a low angle during this period.

events.title_he: Total solar eclipse (not visible from the region)
events.note_he: A total solar eclipse whose path crosses Greenland, Iceland and Spain. Not visible at all from Larkmont.
A partial lunar eclipse; visibility from Larkmont depends on the phase — check moonrise/moonset times for the date.
The best opportunity to see Mercury this year — low in the east before sunrise.

events.title_he: Annular solar eclipse (not visible from the region)
events.note_he: An annular eclipse over South America and the Atlantic Ocean; not visible from the region.

events.title_he: Total solar eclipse — 'eclipse of the century' (partially visible from the region)
events.note_he: The path of totality crosses North Africa and the Middle East (totality lasting up to ~6 minutes near Egypt). From Larkmont a large partial eclipse is expected — the standout sky event of the decade in the region. Eclipse glasses required.
Saturn at opposition; close to the time when the rings appear 'edge-on' (2025) — still nearly closed.
The rings begin to open up again — the view improves year by year.
A total lunar eclipse; check whether totality occurs while the Moon is above the horizon from Larkmont.
A total lunar eclipse; the exact visibility from Larkmont requires checking the event times against moonrise/moonset.

events.title_he: Annular solar eclipse (partially visible from the region)
events.note_he: An annular path crosses Europe and North Africa; from Larkmont a partial eclipse is expected. Eclipse glasses required.

events.title_he: Total solar eclipse (not visible from the region)
events.note_he: A path of totality over southern Africa and Australia; not visible from the region.
